tema: A1 Review — Present Simple: Routines, Frequency, Questions & Likes

banco_oraciones.oracion: Sofía always gets up at six and designs posters every day.
Kenji doesn't understand Colombian jokes, but he always laughs.
Does Carmen cook every day? Yes, she does.
Where does Ben live? He never remembers his own address.
Sofía loves dancing salsa, but she hates getting up late.
Ben usually plays football on Sundays.

banner_diagnostico.opcion_a: Sofía works always in the morning.
banner_diagnostico.opcion_b: Sofía always works in the morning.

inicio.definicion_pragmatica: The present simple, with frequency adverbs, questions and likes, is how we describe the life we live every day — routines, habits and tastes.

ejercicios.pregunta: Ben ___ like mornings. He wakes up late.

ejercicios.afirmacion: Adverbs like 'always' go before the main verb.

ejercicios.instruccion: Turn it into a yes/no question.

ejercicios.pregunta: What do you usually do on Sundays? (I usually...)
What does your best friend love doing? (He/She loves + -ing)

guion: Kenji: Sofía, what time do you get up?
Sofía: I always get up at six. I love the quiet morning.
Kenji: Six?! Ben never gets up before ten.
Sofía: I know! And he doesn't eat breakfast. He just runs to class.
Kenji: What does Carmen do in the morning?
Sofía: She waters her plants and cooks arepas. She never changes her routine.

comprension.pregunta: What time does Sofía get up?
What does Carmen do every morning?
opciones: She waters her plants and cooks
comprension.respuesta: She waters her plants and cooks

reading.texto: Sofía's week is a perfect machine. She always gets up at six, drinks one black coffee and designs until noon. She doesn't work on Sundays — Sundays are for salsa. Kenji admires her discipline, but he doesn't copy it: he studies late and asks questions all day. 'Why does she never arrive late?' he wonders. Ben has a different theory: 'She is not human.' Carmen just smiles. She knows the secret: her granddaughter loves what she does.
